notes: Financial data confirmed via stockanalysis.com: Walmart FY2026 revenue $713,163M vs FY2025 $680,985M yields a delta of ~$32.2B and a growth rate of ~4.72%, matching the original_text's "4.7%". The modified_text substitutes 6.7% for 4.7% while leaving the $32.2B intact, creating an arithmetically inconsistent claim (6.7% on a ~$681B base would imply ~$45.6B, not $32.2B). The report faithfully echoes the modified figure. All five consistency checks and all three classification checks pass. subtlety_rating 4 is plausible: the one-digit change (4.7→6.7) in a percentage buried alongside a correct dollar figure requires knowing the prior-year base to catch, making it moderately subtle but not maximally so.
haiku_reason: The claim is internally consistent mathematically (6.7% of ~$480B base ≈ $32.2B), the citation type is appropriate for such statements (Form 10-K MD&A), and the magnitude is plausible for Walmart's revenue scale. Without access to the source, there are no self-evident red flags indicating misrepresentation.
